---
title: "Emotional places"
format: html
---

## Reflecting on Decisions: Emotional Places

By paying more attention to the decision making process, you will have a greater chance to interrupt the chain of decisions that could lead to a relapse. This is important because it is much easier to stop the process early, before you wind up in a high-risk situation, than later when you are in a situation that is harder to handle and may expose you to a number of triggers. Some times these decisions do not seem directly relevant to drinking, but nonetheless move one closer to drinking. <!--reference irrelevant_decisions.qmd-->


::: callout
### Reflect on the following questions:

- Are there places that bring up mixed emotions for you, such as comfort and sadness, excitement and anxiety, or connection and loneliness?

- What thoughts, memories, or feelings tend to come up when you go to these places?

- Have you noticed any seemingly irrelevant decisions that increase the chances of ending up in emotionally difficult or risky situations?

- What warning signs tell you that being in these environments may be affecting your mood, cravings, or recovery?

- What steps could you take before, during, or after visiting these places to better support your emotional well-being and recovery goals?
:::

![](https://github.com/jjcurtin/aud_support/blob/main/modules/images/emotions3.png?raw=true){fig-align="center"}
